html:
[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 異苑 - Refers to a collection of strange or unusual stories, often categorized under the broader genre of '志怪' (records of strange things).
  • 南朝宋 - Refers to the Southern Dynasty of Song, one of the dynasties in the Southern and Northern Dynasties period.
  • 劉敬叔 - The author of the text, a scholar and writer during the Southern Dynasty of Song.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 異苑 - This term is used to denote a collection of supernatural and extraordinary tales, which were popular during the Southern and Northern Dynasties period. These stories often served as moral and cultural reflections of the time.
  • 南朝宋 - The Southern Dynasty of Song was a significant period in Chinese history, known for its cultural and literary achievements. It was one of the dynasties that ruled the southern part of China during the division of the country into northern and southern regions.
  • 劉敬叔 - He was a prominent figure in the literary circles of his time, known for his contributions to the genre of '志怪' literature.

Proper Nouns

  • 異苑 - The title of the work.
  • 南朝宋 - The dynasty during which the work was written.
  • 劉敬叔 - The author of the work.

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 古語有之曰 - Indicates a traditional saying or proverb, often used to introduce a piece of wisdom or historical anecdote.
  • 荒年菜食而死 - Refers to starving to death during a famine, emphasizing the hardship and scarcity of food.
  • 俱化成青絳 - Means 'both turned into green and purple,' likely referring to a transformation into a form of natural phenomenon, possibly a rainbow.
  • 故俗呼美人虹 - Explains why the phenomenon is called 'beautiful woman rainbow,' suggesting a cultural or folkloric interpretation.
  • 郭云 - A reference to Guo, likely a historical or literary figure, providing additional commentary or explanation.
  • 紅為雩 - Indicates that red is associated with a ritual or ceremony, specifically the雩 (rain-making) ceremony.
  • 俗呼為美人 - Another folkloric reference, indicating that the phenomenon is commonly referred to as 'beautiful woman.'
  • 晉義熙初 - Refers to the early years of the Jinyi era, a period in the Eastern Jin dynasty.
  • 晉陵薛願有虹 - Describes a specific event involving a rainbow in the home of Xue Yuan, a person from Jinling.
  • 飲其釜澳 - Suggests a magical or supernatural interaction with the rainbow.
  • 翕響便竭 - Describes the rapid disappearance of the rainbow.
  • 願輦酒灌之 - Indicates a ritual act to summon or appease the rainbow.
  • 隨投隨涸 - Describes the continuous and immediate effect of the ritual.
  • 吐金滿釜 - Indicates a miraculous outcome.
  • 災弊日祛而豐富歲臻 - Describes the positive impact of the event.
  • 太原溫湛婢見一嫗向婢流涕 - Describes a supernatural encounter.
  • 無孔竅 - Describes the unusual nature of the old woman.
  • 婢駭怖 - Indicates the maid's reaction to the encounter.
  • 告湛 - Indicates the maid's action.
  • 湛遂抽刀逐之 - Indicates Wen Zhan's response.
  • 化成一物如紫虹形 - Describes the transformation.
  • 宛然長舒 - Describes the appearance of the transformed object.
  • 上沒霄漢 - Indicates the height and grandeur of the transformation.
  • 長沙王道憐子義慶在廣陵臥疾 - Describes a specific event involving Wang Dao's son, Yi Qing, who was ill in Guangling.
  • 忽有白虹入室 - Indicates a supernatural occurrence.
  • 就飲其粥 - Suggests a direct interaction with the rainbow.
  • 義慶擲器於階 - Indicates Yi Qing's reaction.
  • 遂作風雨 - Describes the subsequent natural phenomenon.
  • 聲振於庭戶 - Indicates the intensity of the storm.
  • 良久不見 - Indicates the end of the phenomenon.
  • 衡陽山、九嶷山皆有舜廟 - Describes the presence of temples dedicated to Shun on Mount Hengyang and Mount Jiuyi.
  • 每太守修理、祭祀潔敬則聞弦歌之聲 - Means 'whenever the governor repairs and performs a clean and respectful sacrifice, the sound of stringed instruments and singing can be heard,' indicating a miraculous or divine response.
  • 漢章帝時 - Refers to the reign of Emperor Zhang of Han.
  • 零陵文學奚景於冷道縣祠下得笙 - Describes a specific event involving Xiji, a literary figure from Lingling, finding a flute at a temple in Lengdao County.
  • 白玉管 - Means 'white jade tube,' describing the flute.
  • 舜時西王母獻 - Means 'presented by the Queen Mother of the West during Shun's time,' indicating the origin of the flute.
  • 衡山有三峰 - Describes the three peaks of Mount Heng.
  • 極秀 - Means 'extremely beautiful,' describing the peaks.
  • 其一名華蓋，又名紫蓋 - Names the first peak as 'Huagai' and 'Zigai,' indicating its beauty and grandeur.
  • 天景明澈，輒有一雙白鶴回翔其上 - Means 'when the sky is clear and bright, a pair of white cranes often circle above it,' indicating a natural phenomenon.
  • 一峰名石，下有石室 - Names the second peak as 'Shiwei' and describes a stone room at its base.
  • 一峰名芙蓉，最為疏桀 - Names the third peak as 'Furong' and describes it as the most remote and rugged.
  • 自非清霽素朝，不可望見 - Means 'unless it is a clear and bright morning, it cannot be seen,' indicating the difficulty of viewing the peak.
  • 峰上有泉飛氵爪，如一幅絹，分映青林，直注山下 - Describes a waterfall on the peak, comparing it to a piece of silk and describing its descent through the green forest.

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 自投之川 - Literal expression meaning 'threw oneself into the river,' used to describe suicide or a dramatic act of self-sacrifice.
  • 山明水净 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'the mountains are clear and the waters are clean,' often used to depict a serene and beautiful natural setting.
  • 民为立庙 - Means 'the people built a temple,' indicating a place of worship or remembrance.
  • 盘石马迹 - Refers to a flat stone with horse tracks, suggesting a physical remnant of a legendary event.
  • 乘白骥而来 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'came riding a white horse,' often used to describe a heroic or noble figure.
  • 初，桓玄至西下 - Means 'at first, when Huán Xuán came westward,' introducing a historical event.
  • 长啸声 - Refers to a loud, drawn-out sound, often associated with a call or cry.
  • 箕踞石上 - Posture where one sits with legs spread out, often used to describe a relaxed or defiant stance.
  • 自非卒生忘形 - Means 'unless one is completely detached from form,' suggesting a state of spiritual or mental transcendence.
  • 赤城阻其径 - Refers to a red city blocking the path, likely a metaphor for a natural obstacle.
  • 瀑布激其冲 - Means 'waterfalls striking the path,' describing a dramatic natural feature.
  • 莓苔之险 - Refers to the danger posed by moss and algae, suggesting slippery or treacherous terrain.
  • 不测之深 - Means 'depths that cannot be fathomed,' indicating a mysterious or dangerous environment.
  • 弦歌之音 - Refers to the sound of stringed instruments and singing, often associated with a harmonious and pleasant atmosphere.
  • 宫商和畅 - Means 'the tones of the pentatonic scale are harmonious and smooth,' describing a beautiful musical performance.
  • 清弄谐密 - Means 'the clear playing is harmonious and close,' emphasizing the quality of the music.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 屈原 - A famous poet and statesman from the Warring States period, known for his patriotism and tragic end.
  • 桓玄 - A powerful general and regent during the Eastern Jin dynasty, known for his ambition and eventual downfall.
  • 赤城 - A mountain in Zhejiang Province, often associated with spiritual and natural beauty.
  • 天台山 - A famous mountain in Zhejiang Province, known for its scenic beauty and cultural significance.
  • 项籍庙 - A temple dedicated to Xiang Yu, a famous warlord from the late Qin dynasty.
  • 卞王 - A title given to Xiang Yu, meaning 'King of Bian.'
  • 陶侃 - A famous general and statesman from the Eastern Jin dynasty, known for his military prowess and administrative skills.
  • 赤龙 - A mythical creature in Chinese culture, often associated with power and transformation.

Proper Nouns

  • 屈原 - A famous poet and statesman from the Warring States period.
  • 桓玄 - A powerful general and regent during the Eastern Jin dynasty.
  • 赤城 - A mountain in Zhejiang Province.
  • 天台山 - A famous mountain in Zhejiang Province.
  • 项籍 - A famous warlord from the late Qin dynasty.
  • 卞王 - A title given to Xiang Yu.
  • 陶侃 - A famous general and statesman from the Eastern Jin dynasty.
  • 乌程卞山 - A mountain in Wuxing County, Zhejiang Province.
  • 陈郡殷康 - A person from Chen County.
  • 钓矶山 - A mountain where Tao Kan, a famous general, once fished.
  • 乘矶山 - A mountain near the river where a fisherman had a mystical experience.

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 石书 - Refers to inscriptions or books made of stone.
  • 自然石桥 - A naturally formed bridge, possibly a rock formation.
  • 蠢蠢而动 - Describes the movement of a creature as sluggish or slow.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 百丈山 - A mountain in China, likely in a region known for its natural features.
  • 永宁县 - A county in China, possibly in the southern part of the country.
  • 涛山 - A mountain in Yongning County, known for its river and natural formations.
  • 凉州 - An ancient region in northwest China, known for its desert landscapes.
  • 吴孙权赤乌八年 - The 8th year of the Chih Wu era (245 AD) during the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 句容 - A city in Jiangsu Province, known for its historical significance.
  • 东乡太湖 - A lake in the eastern region, possibly in Jiangsu Province.
  • 吴庚申岁 - A specific year in the Chinese zodiac, likely referring to a significant event.

Proper Nouns

  • 百丈山 - Mount Bai Zhang
  • 永宁县 - Yongning County
  • 涛山 - Tao Mountain
  • 凉州 - Liangzhou
  • 吴孙权 - Sun Quan
  • 赤乌八年 - 8th year of the Chih Wu era (245 AD)
  • 校尉陈勋 - General Chen Xun
  • 句容 - Jurong
  • 东乡太湖 - Dongxiang Taihu Lake
  • 吴庚申岁 - Gengshen year

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 永嘉郡有百簿濑 - A place name, '永嘉郡' refers to a region in Zhejiang Province, and '百簿濑' is a specific location within it.
  • 宰生祷祭 - '宰生' means 'slaughtering a living creature,' and '祷祭' means 'praying and sacrificing.'
  • 逾时了无所得 - '逾时' means 'after a long time,' and '了无所得' means 'nothing was obtained.'
  • 众侣忿怨，弃业将罢 - '众侣' means 'the group of people,' '忿怨' means 'angry and resentful,' '弃业' means 'abandoning the business,' and '将罢' means 'about to give up.'
  • 其夕并梦见一老公云 - '其夕' means 'that night,' '并梦' means 'all had a dream,' and '一老公' means 'an old man.'
  • 诸君且可小停，要思其宜 - '诸君' means 'you all,' '且可' means 'may just,' '小停' means 'take a short break,' '要思' means 'think carefully,' and '其宜' means 'what is appropriate.'
  • 夜忽闻有跳跃声 - '夜忽闻' means 'suddenly heard at night,' and '跳跃声' means 'jumping sounds.'
  • 惊起共看 - '惊起' means 'woke up in surprise,' and '共看' means 'looked together.'
  • 乃是大鱼 - '乃是' means 'it turned out to be,' and '大鱼' means 'a big fish.'
  • 以为脍 - '以为' means 'made into,' and '脍' means 'minced meat.'
  • 顿获百簿 - '顿获' means 'suddenly obtained,' and '百簿' means 'a hundred pounds.'
  • 有大鱼化为人 - '有大鱼' means 'a big fish,' '化为人' means 'transformed into a human.'
  • 晚有大鱼攻塞 - '晚' means 'later,' '有大鱼' means 'a big fish,' '攻塞' means 'attacked the barrier.'
  • 须臾 - '须臾' means 'in a moment.'
  • 隶同侣误杀大鱼 - '隶同侣' means 'Wu Li and his companions,' '误杀' means 'mistakenly killed,' and '大鱼' means 'the big fish.'
  • 风雨晦冥 - '风雨' means 'wind and rain,' '晦冥' means 'dark and gloomy.'
  • 鱼悉飞上木间 - '鱼悉' means 'all the fish,' '飞上' means 'flew up,' and '木间' means 'among the trees.'
  • 因号为飞鱼径 - '因号' means 'therefore called,' '为' means 'as,' '飞鱼径' means 'Flying Fish Path.'
  • 山上有井 - '山上有井' means 'there is a well on the mountain.'
  • 鸟巢其中 - '鸟巢' means 'bird's nest,' '其中' means 'inside it.'
  • 金喙黑色而团翅 - '金喙' means 'golden beak,' '黑色' means 'black,' '而' means 'and,' '团翅' means 'round wings.'
  • 此鸟见则大水 - '此鸟' means 'this bird,' '见则' means 'when seen,' '大水' means 'flood.'
  • 井又不可窥 - '井又' means 'the well also,' '不可窥' means 'cannot be looked into.'
  • 窥者不盈一岁辄死 - '窥者' means 'those who look into it,' '不盈一岁' means 'not living for a year,' '辄死' means 'dying immediately.'
  • 浔阳昙椿世居长沙 - '浔阳昙椿' is a person, '世居' means 'lived in,' '长沙' is a city.
  • 宅有古井 - '宅' means 'house,' '有古井' means 'has an ancient well.'
  • 每夜辄闻有如炮竹声 - '每夜' means 'every night,' '辄闻' means 'always hear,' '有如炮竹声' means 'sounds like firecrackers.'
  • 相承谓之龙叱 - '相承' means 'passed down,' '谓之' means 'called it,' '龙叱' means 'dragon's roar.'
  • 句容县有延陵季子庙 - '句容县' is a county in Jiangsu Province, '有延陵季子庙' means 'has the Temple of Yanling Jizi.'
  • 庙前井及渎恒自涌沸 - '庙前井' means 'the well in front of the temple,' '及渎' means 'and the stream,' '恒自涌沸' means 'always boiling up.'
  • 故曰沸井 - '故曰' means 'therefore called,' '沸井' means 'Boiling Well.'
  • 于今犹然 - '于今' means 'even now,' '犹然' means 'still.'
  • 亦曰沸潭 - '亦曰' means 'also called,' '沸潭' means 'Boiling Pond.'
  • 陈郡谢晦字宣明 - '陈郡' is a region, '谢晦' is a person, '字宣明' means 'with the courtesy name Xuanming.'
  • 宅南路上有古井 - '宅南路上' means 'on the road south of the house,' '有古井' means 'has an ancient well.'
  • 以元嘉二年汲者忽见二龙甚分明 - '以元嘉二年' means 'in the second year of the Yuanjia era,' '汲者' means 'those who draw water,' '忽见' means 'suddenly saw,' '二龙' means 'two dragons,' '甚分明' means 'very clearly.'
  • 行道住观，莫不嗟异 - '行道' means 'passersby,' '住观' means 'stopped to watch,' '莫不' means 'all,' '嗟异' means 'expressed amazement.'
  • 有人入井，始知是砖，隐起作龙形 - '有人' means 'someone,' '入井' means 'went into the well,' '始知' means 'found out,' '是砖' means 'it was bricks,' '隐起' means 'rising up,' '作龙形' means 'in the shape of a dragon.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 永嘉郡 - A region in Zhejiang Province during the Eastern Jin dynasty.
  • 晋吴隶 - A person named Wu Li from the Jin dynasty.
  • 兰陵昌虑县 - A county in Shandong Province.
  • 华山 - A mountain in the county.
  • 浔阳昙椿 - A person who lived in Changsha.
  • 长沙 - A city in Hunan Province.
  • 句容县 - A county in Jiangsu Province.
  • 延陵季子庙 - The Temple of Yanling Jizi, a historical figure.
  • 陈郡 - A region in Henan Province.
  • 元嘉二年 - The second year of the Yuanjia era, a period in the Liu Song dynasty.

Proper Nouns

  • 永嘉郡 - A region in Zhejiang Province during the Eastern Jin dynasty.
  • 晋吴隶 - A person named Wu Li from the Jin dynasty.
  • 兰陵昌虑县 - A county in Shandong Province.
  • 华山 - A mountain in the county.
  • 浔阳昙椿 - A person who lived in Changsha.
  • 长沙 - A city in Hunan Province.
  • 句容县 - A county in Jiangsu Province.
  • 延陵季子庙 - The Temple of Yanling Jizi, a historical figure.
  • 陈郡 - A region in Henan Province.
  • 元嘉二年 - The second year of the Yuanjia era, a period in the Liu Song dynasty.

[6]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 元嘉初 - Refers to the early years of the reign of Emperor Wen of Song, specifically the Yuanjia era (424-453 AD).
  • 武溪蛮 - Refers to a group of non-Han Chinese people living in the Wuxi area, likely referring to the Miao or Yao ethnic groups.
  • 豁然开朗 - A classical idiom meaning 'suddenly becoming bright and clear,' often used to describe a sudden realization or a pleasant surprise.
  • 桑果蔚然 - Describes a lush and abundant scene of mulberry trees and fruit trees.
  • 行人翱翔 - Refers to people moving about freely and comfortably.
  • 项中复有面 - Describes a person with another face on their neck, a mythical or supernatural phenomenon.
  • 耆老云 - Refers to elderly people who say or report something.
  • 错令轻则韵乖 - Means 'if you make it lighter, the sound will be out of tune.'
  • 钟鸣应之耳 - Means 'the bell is responding to it,' suggesting a supernatural connection.
  • 洛钟宫商相应 - Refers to the musical tuning of the palace bell in Luoyang, where the '宫' (gong) and '商' (shang) tones are in harmony.
  • 朝暮撞钟 - Means 'striking the bell in the morning and evening,' a common practice in ancient Chinese rituals.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 元嘉 - The era name of Emperor Wen of Song, indicating the historical period.
  • 武溪蛮 - Refers to a specific group of non-Han Chinese people in the Wuxi region.
  • 毋丘俭 - A prominent official during the Three Kingdoms period, known for his military campaigns.
  • 吴郡临平岸 - Refers to a location in Wu County, near the mouth of the Yangtze River.
  • 毋丘俭字仲恭 - His courtesy name, which is a common practice in ancient China.
  • 晋武帝 - Refers to Emperor Sima Yan of the Jin dynasty, who unified China after the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 张华 - A famous scholar and statesman of the Jin dynasty, known for his wisdom and knowledge.
  • 洛钟 - The palace bell in Luoyang, a significant symbol of imperial authority and ritual.

Proper Nouns

  • 元嘉 - The era name of Emperor Wen of Song.
  • 武溪蛮 - A group of non-Han Chinese people.
  • 毋丘俭 - A historical figure from the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 吴郡临平岸 - A location in Wu County.
  • 晋武帝 - Emperor Sima Yan of the Jin dynasty.
  • 张华 - A famous scholar and statesman of the Jin dynasty.
  • 洛钟 - The palace bell in Luoyang.
  • 毋丘俭字仲恭 - His courtesy name.
  • 晋中朝 - Refers to the central government during the Jin dynasty.

[7]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 豫章有石黄 - Descriptive phrase indicating that the region of Yuzhang (modern-day Nanchang) has a type of stone called 'shihuang'.
  • 白色而理疏 - Describes the stone as white in color and having a loose or porous texture.
  • 以水灌之便热 - Literal expression meaning that when water is poured over the stone, it becomes hot.
  • 加鼎于上，炊足以熟 - Literal expression meaning that placing a tripod (鼎) on top of the stone and cooking food will make it ready.
  • 冷则灌之 - Literal expression meaning that when the stone cools down, it should be poured with water again.
  • 此燃石也 - Literal expression meaning 'this is a burning stone'.
  • 元康中有人入嵩高山下得竹简一枚 - Descriptive phrase indicating that during the Yuan Kang period, someone entered the foot of Mount Song and found a bamboo slip.
  • 上有两行科斗书 - Literal expression meaning that there were two lines of script on the bamboo slip.
  • 台中外传以相示 - Literal expression meaning that officials in the palace passed the bamboo slip around to show each other.
  • 莫有知者 - Literal expression meaning 'no one knew'.
  • 司空张华以问博士东 - Literal expression meaning that Zhang Hua, the Grand Works Minister, asked the scholar Dong .
  • 此明帝显节陵中策文也 - Literal expression meaning 'this is the inscription from the Xianjie Tomb of Emperor Ming'.
  • 检校果然 - Literal expression meaning 'upon checking, it turned out to be true'.
  • 晋惠帝元康五年，武库火 - Descriptive phrase indicating that in the fifth year of the Yuan Kang period of Emperor Huai of Jin, the armory caught fire.
  • 烧汉高祖斩白蛇剑、孔子履、王莽头等三物 - Literal expression meaning that the armory fire burned three items: the sword of Han Gaozu (Emperor Gao) that cut the white snake, the shoes of Confucius, and the head of Wang Mang.
  • 中书监张茂先惧难作，列兵陈卫 - Literal expression meaning that Zhang Maoxian, the Chief Clerk of the Secretariat, feared trouble and arranged soldiers for protection.
  • 咸见此剑穿屋飞去，莫知所向 - Literal expression meaning that everyone saw the sword pass through the roof and fly away, and no one knew where it went.
  • 晋康帝建元中，有渔父垂钓，得一金锁 - Descriptive phrase indicating that during the Jianyuan period of Emperor Kang of Jin, a fisherman caught a golden lock while fishing.
  • 引锁尽，见金牛 - Literal expression meaning that when the fisherman pulled the lock out, he saw a golden ox.
  • 急挽出牛，断，犹得锁长二尺 - Literal expression meaning that he quickly pulled out the ox, which broke, leaving a lock two feet long.
  • 晋太元中，桂阳临武徐孙江行，见岸有钱溢出 - Descriptive phrase indicating that during the Taoyuan period of Emperor Xiaowu of Jin, Xu Sunjiang from Linwu, Guiyang, saw money overflowing from the bank.
  • 即辇着船中，须臾悉变成土 - Literal expression meaning that he immediately loaded it onto his boat, and soon it all turned into earth.
  • 晋义熙中，庞猗为宜都太守，御人牧马于野 - Descriptive phrase indicating that during the Yixi period of Emperor An of Jin, Pang Yi served as the Prefect of Yidu and kept people and horses in the fields.
  • 见一铜炉，上舀带锁而行 - Literal expression meaning that he saw a copper stove with a handle and a lock, moving.
  • 持归以呈猗 - Literal expression meaning that he brought it back to show Pang Yi.
  • 遂槛盛逸下荆州无都北乃 - Literal expression meaning that he then placed it in a cage and sent it to the north of Jingzhou, which was not the capital.
  • 忽风雨，有叫声，火光烛天，径来趋船，失炉所在 - Literal expression meaning that suddenly there was wind and rain, with a sound, and fire lit up the sky, coming straight to the ship, and the stove was lost.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 薯蓣一名山芋 - A compound name for a plant, indicating that '薯蓣' is also known as '山芋' (mountain yam). This is a typical classical naming convention.
  • 默然则获，唱名者便不可得 - A metaphorical expression meaning that if one remains silent, they will succeed in obtaining the plant, but if they call out its name, it will be lost.
  • 人有植者，随所种之物而像之也 - A metaphor suggesting that when someone plants this plant, it will resemble the thing it was planted with.
  • 人参一名土精 - Another compound name, indicating that '人参' (ginseng) is also known as '土精' (earth spirit).
  • 昔，有人掘之，始下铧，便闻土中呻吟声 - '昔' is a classical time marker meaning 'in ancient times.' '始下铧' refers to the beginning of digging with a plow or spade, and '呻吟声' is a sound of moaning or groaning.
  • 交州诸菌以叶涂人躯，便举体菌生 - A metaphorical or magical expression: if the leaves of certain mushrooms from Jiaozhou are applied to a person’s body, the entire body becomes covered in mushrooms.
  • 生既遍，便就朽烂，肌肉消腐 - Once the mushrooms have fully grown, the body decays and the flesh rots away.
  • 隋县永阳有山，壁立千仞 - A classical expression describing a mountain that rises steeply and is thousands of feet high.
  • 岩上有石室，古名为神农窟 - A reference to a stone chamber on the cliff, traditionally called the 'Shennong Cave.'
  • 窟前有百药丛茂，莫不毕备 - A metaphorical expression meaning that in front of the cave, a variety of medicinal herbs grow in abundance, and all are present.
  • 又别有异物藤花，形似菱菜，朝紫中绿，晡黄暮青，夜赤五色迭耀 - A detailed description of a strange plant with flowers that change color throughout the day.

  • 隋县永阳 - A place name, likely referring to a mountainous region in ancient China. '隋县' may refer to a county under the Sui Dynasty.
  • 神农窟 - The 'Shennong Cave,' named after Shennong, the legendary Divine Farmer.
  • 百药丛茂 - Refers to the abundance of medicinal herbs, a common motif in Chinese herbal lore and mythology.
  • 晋太康二年 - The second year of the Taikang era of the Western Jin Dynasty (281 CE).
  • 南洲 - A general term for the southern regions of China, possibly referring to a specific area or a mythical location.
  • 白鹤 - White cranes are symbolic in Chinese culture, often associated with longevity, purity, and the supernatural.
  • 鸾 - A mythical bird, often associated with the phoenix or a rare and noble bird.
  • 镜照之 - The use of a mirror to reflect the image of the bird is a metaphor for the bird recognizing its own kind.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 还辄说僮仆善恶 - A set phrase meaning 'whenever he returned, he would tell about the good and bad of the servants,' indicating a habit of Zhang Hua.
  • 见藏瓮中，何由得知 - A rhetorical question expressing the parrot's inability to speak, literally 'It was hidden in a jar, how could it know?'
  • 令唤鹦鹉 - An instruction, literally 'He ordered the parrot to be called.'
  • 昨夜梦恶，不宜出户 - The parrot's response to being called, literally 'Last night I had a bad dream, it's not suitable to go out.'
  • 至庭为所搏 - Describes the parrot's fate, literally 'It went to the courtyard and was attacked by a hawk.'
  • 教其啄脚，仅而获免 - Shows Zhang Hua's quick thinking, literally 'He taught it to peck at the hawk's leg, and it barely escaped.'
  • 虽乐，不可久也 - The parrot's reasoning for leaving, literally 'Although it was happy, it couldn't stay long.'
  • 后数月，山中大火 - Sets the scene for the parrot's heroic act, literally 'After several months, a big fire broke out in the mountain.'
  • 汝虽有志意，何足云也 - A dismissive response from the deity, literally 'You may have good intentions, but what is that worth?'
  • 虽知不能救，然尝侨居是山，禽兽行善，皆为兄弟，不忍见耳 - The parrot's explanation, literally 'Although I know I can't save them, I have lived here as a guest, and the birds and beasts here are like brothers, I can't bear to see them.'
  • 天神嘉感，即为灭火 - Shows the positive outcome, literally 'The deity was moved and immediately put out the fire.'
  • 五月五日剪鸲鹆舌 - Introduces a new story, literally 'On the fifth day of the fifth month, the tongue of a magpie was cut.'
  • 参军善弹琵琶，鸲鹆每听辄移时 - Shows the magpie's interest in music, literally 'The military officer was good at playing the pipa, and the magpie would listen for a long time.'
  • 山鸡爱其毛羽，映水则舞 - Describes the peafowl's behavior, literally 'The peafowl loved its feathers and would dance when it saw its reflection in the water.'
  • 魏武时南方献之 - Introduces the peafowl to the emperor, literally 'During the time of Wei Wu, the south presented it to him.'
  • 帝欲其鸣舞而无由 - Sets up the problem, literally 'The emperor wanted it to sing and dance but had no way to do so.'
  • 公子苍舒令置大镜其前，鸡鉴形而舞，不知止，遂乏死 - Describes the peafowl's tragic end, literally 'The young lord Cangshu ordered a large mirror to be placed in front of it, and the peafowl saw its reflection and danced, not knowing when to stop, and eventually died.'
  • 韦仲将为之赋其事 - Indicates the literary response to the event, literally 'Wei Zhongjiang wrote a poem about the incident.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 晋义熙三年 - Indicates the year 407 AD during the Jin Dynasty, specifically the reign of Emperor An.
  • 婢炊衤 - Describes a female servant cooking, where '婢' means servant and '炊衤' refers to cooking or preparing food.
  • 忽有群鸟集灶 - Means 'suddenly a group of birds gathered around the stove,' indicating an unusual occurrence.
  • 竞来啄啖 - Describes the birds coming to peck and eat, emphasizing their sudden and aggressive behavior.
  • 驱逐不去 - Means 'unable to drive away,' indicating the persistence of the birds despite attempts to remove them.
  • 咋杀两鸟 - Means 'bit and killed two birds,' describing the actions of the dog.
  • 余鸟因共咋杀犬 - Means 'the remaining birds then bit and killed the dog,' indicating a retaliatory action.
  • 又啖其肉，唯余骨存 - Means 'then they ate its meat, leaving only bones,' describing the aftermath of the attack.
  • 始阳相催而鸣 - Means 'beginning to call each other to sing,' indicating the start of the breeding season for the cuckoo.
  • 先鸣者吐血死 - Means 'those who sing first will die spitting blood,' indicating a fatal consequence for early singers.
  • 常有人山行 - Means 'there was once a man who was traveling in the mountains,' setting the scene for a supernatural event.
  • 见一群寂然 - Means 'saw a group of birds sitting quietly,' describing the initial observation.
  • 聊学其声 - Means 'tried to imitate their sound,' indicating the man's attempt to mimic the birds.
  • 使呕血死 - Means 'caused him to vomit blood and die,' indicating the fatal consequence of the imitation.
  • 初鸣，先听其声者，主离别 - Means 'when they first sing, those who hear them first will experience separation,' indicating a superstitious belief.
  • 厕上听其声不祥 - Means 'hearing their sound in the toilet is inauspicious,' indicating a specific place where hearing the sound is considered bad luck.
  • 厌之法 - Means 'a method to ward off evil,' indicating a ritual or practice to counteract negative effects.
  • 当为大声以应之 - Means 'one should make a loud sound to respond to them,' indicating the recommended action.
  • 晋兖州刺史沛国宋处宗 - Refers to Cao Cao's official, Song Chuzong, who lived in the state of Pei.
  • 尝买得一长鸣鸡 - Means 'once bought a rooster that could crow for a long time,' indicating the rooster's unique ability.
  • 爱养甚至 - Means 'took great care of it,' indicating the level of care and affection.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This sequence describes the dog killing two birds, the remaining birds killing the dog, and the birds eating the dog's meat.
  • This sequence describes the cuckoos calling each other to sing, with the first singers dying from spitting blood.
  • This sequence describes a man traveling in the mountains, seeing a group of birds, imitating their sound, and dying from vomiting blood.
  • This clause describes the superstition that hearing the cuckoos' first call predicts separation.
  • This clause describes the superstition that hearing the cuckoos' call in the toilet is inauspicious.
  • This clause describes the method to ward off evil by making a loud sound.
  • This long sequence describes Song Chuzong's experience with the talking rooster and its impact on his understanding of profound language.
  • This sequence describes Yao Bi taking bricks from the underground canal in Luoyang, finding a pair of golden geese, and raising them in the canal.
  • This sequence describes Fu Cheng losing a pair of geese for three years and then suddenly having more than thirty geese led to his house.
  • This sequence describes Yu De seeing a supernatural sign, reacting to it, and then seeing a second sign, repeating the process three times.
  • This sequence describes Yu De telling Xia Man about the events, preparing to wait with weapons, and then killing a tiger that arrived.
  • This clause refers to Huang Qi, a fellow villager, who also reported similar events.

  • 吳孫皓時，臨海得毛人 - Literal meaning 'during the reign of Sun Hao of Wu, a hairy person was found by the sea.'
  • 《山海經》云 - Literal meaning 'The *Classic of Mountains and Seas* says.'
  • 山精如人而有毛 - Literal meaning 'the mountain spirit looks like a human but has hair.'
  • 此蒋山精也 - Literal meaning 'this is the mountain spirit of Mount Jiang.'
  • 故《抱朴子》曰 - Literal meaning 'therefore, *Baopu Zi* says.'
  • 山之精，形如小儿而独足 - Literal meaning 'the mountain spirit has the shape of a child and only one foot.'

  • 其名曰支 - Literal meaning 'its name is *Zhi*.'
  • 知而呼之，即当自却耳 - Literal meaning 'if you know its name and call it, it will retreat on its own.'
  • 一名曰超空，可燕呼之 - Literal meaning 'another name is *Chao Kong*, which can be called in a relaxed manner.'
  • 又或如鼓，赤色，一足，其名曰浑 - Literal meaning 'or it might look like a drum, red in color, with one foot, and its name is *Hun*.'
  • 又或如人，长九尺，衣裘戴笠，名曰金累 - Literal meaning 'or it might look like a human, nine feet tall, wearing fur and a hat, and its name is *Jinlei*.'
  • 又或如龙，有五色赤角，名曰飞龙 - Literal meaning 'or it might look like a dragon, with five colors and red horns, and its name is *Feilong*.'
  • 见之皆可呼其名，不敢为害 - Literal meaning 'when you see them, you can call their names, and they will not harm you.'
  • 《玄中记》山精如人，一足长三四尺，食山蟹，夜出昼藏 - Literal meaning 'The *Xuanzhong Ji* says the mountain spirit looks like a human, has one foot, is three to four feet long, eats mountain crabs, comes out at night, and hides during the day.'

  • 故以为名 - Therefore, it is named after a bird.
  • 常脱壳而游 - It often leaves its shell to move around.
  • 朝出则有虫，类如蜘蛛，入其壳中螺 - In the morning, it leaves its shell, and a spider-like creature enters the shell.
  • 夕还则此虫出 - In the evening, the creature returns to the shell.
  • 庾阐所谓鹦鹉内游，寄居负壳者也 - As stated by Yu Chan, the nautilus is like a parrot that lives inside its shell.
  • 晋明帝尝欲肆{生月}闭而不谋 - Emperor Ming of Jin once wanted to do something without consulting others.
  • 乃屏曲室，去左右，下帷草诏 - He secluded himself in a small room, dismissed his attendants, and drafted an edict behind a curtain.
  • 有大苍蝇触帐而入，萃于笔端 - A large green fly touched the curtain and flew into the room, landing on the tip of the brush.
  • 须臾亡去 - Soon after, it flew away.
  • 帝窃异焉 - The emperor secretly found this strange.
  • 令人寻看 - Ordered someone to look for it.
  • 即蝇所集处辄传有诏 - Wherever the fly had landed, it was said that an edict had been issued.
  • 喧然已遍矣 - The news spread quickly.
  • 有小虫形色如大豆 - A small insect with the color and shape of a soybean.
  • 咒令叩头，又咒令吐血 - Chanted spells to make it bow its head and then to make it spit blood.
  • 皆从所教 - It followed the instructions.
  • 如似请放稽颡 - It seemed to be asking for forgiveness.
  • 辄七十而有声 - It made a sound after seventy times.
  • 故俗呼为叩头虫也 - Therefore, it is commonly called the 'bowing bug.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 一名 - Classical expression meaning 'also known as' or 'another name for.'
  • 恒吐丝自悬 - Literal phrase meaning 'constantly spitting silk to hang itself,' describing the behavior of the insect.
  • 昔齐东郭姜既乱崔杼之室 - Complex phrase referring to a historical event where Lady Dongguo of Qi disrupted the household of Cui Zhu. '昔' means 'in the past,' '既' means 'already,' and '乱' means 'to disrupt' or 'to disturb.'
  • 庆封杀其二子 - Historical reference where King Qing of Qi killed two of Lady Dongguo's sons. '杀' means 'to kill,' and '其二子' means 'her two sons.'
  • 姜亦自经 - Phrase meaning 'Lady Dongguo also hanged herself.' '自经' is a euphemism for suicide by hanging.
  • 俗传此妇骸化为虫 - Phrase meaning 'it is said that the bones of this woman transformed into an insect.' '俗传' means 'it is said' or 'according to popular belief.'
  • 以缢女名虫 - Phrase meaning 'and the insect was named after the hanged woman.' '以...名' means 'to name after.'
  • 蜀郡临邛县有火井 - Phrase meaning 'in Linqiong County of Shu Commandery, there is a fire well.' '火井' refers to a natural gas well that produces flames.
  • 汉室之隆则炎赫弥炽 - Phrase meaning 'when the Han dynasty was at its height, the flames burned even more fiercely.' '隆' means 'prosperous' or 'flourishing,' '炎赫' means 'fiery' or 'burning brightly,' and '弥炽' means 'even more intense.'
  • 暨桓灵之际，火势渐微 - Phrase meaning 'by the time of Emperors Huan and Ling, the fire had become less intense.' '暨' means 'by the time of,' '桓灵' refers to Emperors Huan and Ling of the Eastern Han dynasty, and '渐微' means 'gradually weakened.'
  • 诸葛亮一瞰而更盛 - Phrase meaning 'when Zhuge Liang took a look at it, the fire became even stronger.' '一瞰' means 'to take a look,' and '更盛' means 'became even stronger.'
  • 至景曜元年，人以烛投即灭 - Phrase meaning 'by the first year of Jingyao, when people threw candles into it, the fire went out.' '景曜元年' refers to the first year of the Jingyao era during the Shu Han dynasty, and '即灭' means 'went out immediately.'
  • 其年蜀并于魏 - Phrase meaning 'that year, Shu was annexed by Wei.' '并于' means 'was annexed by.'
  • 孙钟富春人，坚父也 - Phrase meaning 'Sun Zhong was from Fuchun and was the father of Sun Jian.' '富春' is the place name Fuchun, and '坚父' means 'father of Sun Jian.'
  • 种瓜为业 - Phrase meaning 'he made a living growing melons.' '种瓜' means 'growing melons,' and '为业' means 'as a profession.'
  • 诣钟乞瓜 - Phrase meaning 'came to Sun Zhong to beg for melons.' '诣' means 'to visit,' and '乞' means 'to beg.'
  • 礼敬殷勤 - Phrase meaning 'with great respect and hospitality.' '礼敬' means 'respect,' and '殷勤' means 'hospitality' or 'kindness.'
  • 司命郎 - Term referring to officials who manage life and death, often associated with deities or spirits.
  • 感君接见之厚 - Phrase meaning 'grateful for your generous reception.' '感' means 'grateful,' '君' means 'you,' '接见' means 'reception,' and '厚' means 'generous.'
  • 欲连世封侯 - Phrase meaning 'wanting to be enfeoffed as a marquis for generations.' '连世' means 'for generations,' and '封侯' means 'to be enfeoffed as a marquis.'
  • 欲数世天子 - Phrase meaning 'wanting to have emperors for several generations.' '数世' means 'several generations,' and '天子' means 'emperor.'
  • 因为钟定墓地 - Phrase meaning 'they then designated a burial site for Sun Zhong.' '因为' means 'therefore,' and '定墓地' means 'designate a burial site.'
  • 出门悉化成白鹊 - Phrase meaning 'as they left, everything turned into white magpies.' '出门' means 'as they left,' and '悉化成' means 'all turned into.'
  • 一云 - Phrase meaning 'another version says.' '一云' is a common phrase used to introduce alternative accounts or legends.
  • 孙坚丧父，行葬地 - Phrase meaning 'Sun Jian, after his father's death, went to choose a burial site.' '丧父' means 'after his father's death,' and '行葬地' means 'went to choose a burial site.'
  • 忽有一人曰 - Phrase meaning 'suddenly, a man appeared and said.' '忽' means 'suddenly,' and '曰' means 'said.'
  • 君欲百世诸侯乎？欲四世帝乎？ - Phrase meaning 'Do you want to be a feudal lord for a hundred generations? Or do you want to have emperors for four generations?' '百世' means 'a hundred generations,' '诸侯' means 'feudal lords,' '四世' means 'four generations,' and '帝' means 'emperor.'
  • 此人因指一处 - Phrase meaning 'the man then pointed to a place.' '因指' means 'then pointed,' and '一处' means 'a place.'
  • 喜悦而没 - Phrase meaning 'he was delighted and disappeared.' '喜悦' means 'delighted,' and '没' means 'disappeared.'
  • 异而从之 - Phrase meaning 'Sun Jian found it strange and followed him.' '异' means 'found strange,' and '从之' means 'followed him.'
  • 时富春有沙涨暴出 - Phrase meaning 'at that time, a sandbar suddenly emerged in Fuchun.' '沙涨' means 'sandbar,' and '暴出' means 'suddenly emerged.'
  • 及坚为监丞，邻党相送于上 - Phrase meaning 'when Sun Jian became a supervisor, his neighbors and relatives accompanied him up.' '监丞' means 'supervisor,' and '邻党相送于上' means 'neighbors and relatives accompanied him up.'
  • 父老谓曰 - Phrase meaning 'the elders said to him.' '父老' means 'elders,' and '谓曰' means 'said to him.'
  • 此沙狭而长子，后将为长沙矣 - Phrase meaning 'this narrow and long sandbar will become Changsha in the future.' '狭而长子' means 'narrow and long,' and '后将为长沙矣' means 'will become Changsha in the future.'
  • 果起义兵于长沙 - Phrase meaning 'indeed, he raised an army in Changsha.' '果' means 'indeed,' and '起义兵' means 'raised an army.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 隐居管涔之山 - Refers to Liu Yao living in seclusion in the Guan Cen Mountains, a specific place in Shanxi Province.
  • 夜中忽有一童子入跪曰 - Describes a sudden appearance of a child at night, which is a common motif in Chinese folklore and legends.
  • 管涔王使小臣奉谒赵皇帝 - Indicates that the child is a messenger from the King of Guan Cen Mountain, paying respects to the Zhao Emperor.
  • 献剑一口 - Refers to the act of presenting a sword, a significant gesture in Chinese culture.
  • 置前再拜而去 - Describes the ritual of placing the sword before the emperor and bowing twice before leaving.
  • 以烛视之 - Means to examine the sword with a candle, emphasizing its importance and the careful inspection.
  • 剑长二尺，光泽非常，赤玉为饰，背有铭云 - Describes the sword's physical characteristics, including its length, shine, decoration, and inscription.
  • 神剑服御除众毒 - The inscription on the sword, meaning 'Divine Sword for Wearing and Removing All Poisons.'
  • 石勒为郭敬客时 - Refers to Shi Le being a guest of Guo Jing, a historical figure.
  • 襄国有谶曰 - Indicates a prophecy or omen in Xiangguo (modern-day Xiangfen, Shanxi).
  • 力在左，革在右，让无言，或入口 - The prophecy, which is cryptic and requires interpretation.
  • 让去言为襄字，或入口乃国字也 - Explanation of the prophecy, where '让' (let) minus '言' (speech) becomes '襄' (Xiang), and '或' (or) plus '口' (mouth) becomes '国' (guo, country).
  • 石勒伐刘曜于洛阳 - Describes Shi Le's military campaign against Liu Yao in Luoyang.
  • 从大河南济 - Refers to crossing the Yellow River.
  • 时河冻，将合军至而水自泮 - Describes the river freezing and then thawing just as the army arrives.
  • 舟楫无阂 - Means the boats had no obstacles, indicating smooth passage.
  • 遂生擒曜，谓是神灵之助 - States that Shi Le captured Liu Yao alive, attributing the success to divine assistance.
  • 命曰灵昌津 - Names the crossing point 'Lingchang Ferry.'
  • 晋时长安谣曰 - Refers to a rhyme or song in Chang'an during the Jin Dynasty.
  • 秦川城中血没 - Describes a scene of blood covering the streets, indicating a violent event.
  • 惟有凉州倚柱看 - Means only the people in Liangzhou (modern-day Gansu) were able to observe the events calmly.
  • 及惠愍之间 - Refers to the period of Emperor Huizong and Emperor Min of Jin, a time of chaos and war.
  • 关内歼破 - Describes the destruction and devastation within the passes.
  • 浮血飘舟 - Means the blood floated on the river, indicating the severity of the violence.
  • 张轨拥众一方，威恩共著 - States that Zhang Gui, a local leader, maintained both authority and benevolence.
  • 凉州张骏字公彦 - Introduces Zhang Jun, a notable figure in Liangzhou.
  • 九年天雨五谷于武威，炖煌，植之悉生，因名天麦 - Describes a miraculous event where grains rained down in Wuwei and Dunhuang, and they all grew, leading to the name 'Heavenly Wheat.'
  • 凉州张祚伪和平中有神见于玄武殿，自称玄冥，与人言语 - Describes a divine figure appearing in the Xuanwu Hall, identifying himself as Xuanming and speaking with people.
  • 祚日夜祈之，神言与之福利，祚甚信之 - States that Zhang Zuo prayed to the divine figure daily, and the figure promised him good fortune, which Zhang Zuo believed.

  • 龟三千六百岁而终，终必妖兴，亡国之征也 - Indicates that the turtle, having lived for 3600 years, predicted that its death would bring disaster and the downfall of the state.

  • 侃以针挑令彻，血流弹壁，乃作公字。 - Tao Kan used a needle to prick it, and blood flowed onto the wall, forming the character 'gong' (公).

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 后羡之随亲之县，住在县内 - Means 'later Xianzhi followed his parents to the county and lived inside it,' setting up the context for the next event.
  • 尝暂出而贼自后破县 - Means 'once he briefly left and the bandits broke the county behind him,' describing a dangerous situation.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 沙门释慧远栖神庐岳 - 沙门指佛教僧人，释慧远是僧人的名字，栖神意为居住在精神状态中，庐岳指庐山，中国著名山脉。
  • 常有游龙翔其前 - 游龙比喻龙在僧人面前飞舞或移动，象征着超自然或神圣的存在。
  • 远公有奴 - 远公是对僧人的尊称，有奴表示他有一个仆人。
  • 有石掷中，乃腾跃上升 - 有石掷中指石头被投掷并击中某物，乃腾跃上升描述了突然的跳跃或上升。
  • 有顷，风云飚□ - 有顷指一会儿之后，风云指风和云，飚□是一个不完整的词语，可能指突然的风暴或旋风。
  • 公知是龙之所兴 - 公是对僧人的尊称，知指知道，是龙之所兴指龙的活动或显现。
  • 登山烧香，会僧齐声唱偈 - 登山指爬山，烧香是烧香的仪式，会僧指聚集僧侣，齐声唱偈指一起唱诵佛教偈语。
  • 于是霹雳回向投龙之石 - 于是指因此，霹雳指雷电，回向指转向，投龙之石指投向龙的石头。
  • 沙门竺慧炽，新野人 - 沙门指佛教僧人，竺慧炽是僧人的名字，新野人表示他是来自新野的地方人。
  • 住江陵四层佛寺 - 住指居住，江陵是地名，四层佛寺指四层的佛教寺庙。
  • 永初二年卒 - 永初二年指永初时期的第二年（398-401年），卒指去世。
  • 弟子为设七日会 - 弟子指僧人的弟子，为设指安排或举行，七日会指七天的纪念会。
  • 其日将夕，烧香竟 - 其日将夕指当天傍晚，烧香指烧香的仪式，竟指完成。
  • 沙门道贤因往视炽弟子 - 沙门道贤是另一个僧人的名字，因往视指去拜访，炽弟子指已故僧人的弟子。
  • 至房前，忽暖暧若人形 - 至房前指到达房间前，忽暖暧描述突然的温暖或模糊的外观，若人形指像人形。
  • 详视乃慧炽也 - 详视指仔细看，乃慧炽也指确认是已故僧人。
  • 容貌衣服不异生时 - 容貌指外貌，衣服指衣物，不异生时指与生前无异。
  • 谓贤曰：“君且食肉美否？” - 谓指说，贤是对僧人的尊称，君且食肉美否指“你吃的肉好吃吗？”
  • 曰“美” - 曰指说，美指好吃或美味。
  • 炽曰：“我生不能断肉，今落饿鬼地狱。” - 炽曰指已故僧人说，我生不能断肉指“我生前无法戒除肉食”，今落饿鬼地狱指“现在我堕入饿鬼地狱”。
  • 道贤惧，未及得答 - 道贤惧指僧人感到害怕，未及得答指没有时间回答。
  • 炽复言：“汝若不信，试看我背后。” - 炽复言指已故僧人再次说，汝若不信指“如果你不相信”，试看我背后指“试着看看我的背后”。
  • 乃回背示贤 - 乃回背指转过身，示贤指给僧人看。
  • 见三黄狗形，半似驴，眼甚赤光，照户内 - 见三黄狗形指看到三个黄色的狗形，半似驴指半像驴，眼甚赤光指眼睛非常红光，照户内指照亮房间。
  • 状欲啮炽而复止，贤骇怖闷绝，良久乃苏 - 状欲啮炽而复止指它们似乎想咬僧人但又停止，贤骇怖闷绝指僧人感到极度恐惧并昏厥，良久乃苏指过了一会儿才苏醒。

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 尝自京归 - Means 'once returned from the capital,' indicating a journey.
  • 未至二十里许 - Means 'not quite twenty li away,' where '许' is a measure word indicating an approximate distance.
  • 天暗失道 - Means 'the sky grew dark and he lost his way,' describing the circumstances of the journey.
  • 遥望火光 - Means 'looked far and saw a light,' indicating the discovery of a place to stay.
  • 女弱独居，不得宿客 - Means 'a weak woman lives alone and cannot accommodate guests,' a polite refusal.
  • 欲进路，碍夜不可前去 - Means 'wants to continue the journey, but it is too dark to proceed,' explaining the need for shelter.
  • 乞寄外住 - Means 'asks to stay outside,' a request to be accommodated.
  • 女然之 - Means 'the woman agreed,' a concise expression of consent.
  • 以此女独居一室 - Means 'since the woman lives alone in a room,' providing context for the situation.
  • 虑其夫至 - Means 'fearing her husband might arrive,' a concern about potential interruptions.
  • 何以过嫌 - Means 'why be overly suspicious,' a rhetorical question to ease concerns.
  • 保无虞，不相误也 - Means 'I assure you there is no danger, and I will not mislead you,' a reassurance.
  • 为树设食 - Means 'prepared food for Qin Shu,' indicating hospitality.
  • 食物悉是陈久 - Means 'all the food was old and stale,' describing the quality of the meal.
  • 承未出适 - Means 'since you have not yet married,' a reference to the unmarried state.
  • 欲结大义 - Means 'wants to form a serious relationship,' a euphemism for marriage.
  • 能相顾否 - Means 'can you consider me?' a formal way of asking for a relationship.
  • 自顾鄙薄 - Means 'I consider myself unworthy,' a humble self-assessment.
  • 岂足伉俪 - Means 'how could I be worthy of a spouse?' a rhetorical question expressing humility.
  • 遂与寝止 - Means 'they then slept together,' a concise description of the intimate encounter.
  • 向晨 - Means 'early morning,' indicating the time of departure.
  • 俱起执别 - Means 'they both got up and took leave,' a formal farewell.
  • 与君一睹，后面无期 - Means 'this is our last meeting, we may never see each other again,' a poignant farewell.
  • 以指环一双赠之 - Means 'gave him a pair of finger rings,' a gesture of affection.
  • 结置衣带 - Means 'tied them to his belt,' a way of keeping a memento.
  • 顾其宿处，乃是冢墓 - Means 'looked back at where he stayed, and it turned out to be a tomb,' a shocking revelation.
  • 亡其指环 - Means 'lost the finger rings,' indicating the disappearance of the gift.
  • 结带如故 - Means 'the belt ties remained as before,' a detail that adds to the mystery.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 老姥 - A term for an elderly woman or grandmother; carries connotation of age and possibly rural or traditional setting.
  • 纺绩 - Compound word meaning 'spinning and weaving,' referring to textile production.
  • 钅奏 - Character with radical '钅' (metal) and phonetic component '奏'; likely a misspelling or variant of '针' (zhēn), meaning 'needle.'
  • 何物鬼担去 - Colloquial expression meaning 'What kind of ghost carried it away?' Metaphorical expression of frustration or surprise.
  • 宜就觅之 - Polite suggestion meaning 'You should look for it [yourself].'
  • 独骂云 - 'Muttered alone,' indicating the woman is speaking to herself.
  • 临终执乞手 - 'Held his hand at the time of death,' common expression in classical Chinese for a dying person holding someone’s hand in farewell or final words.
  • 再婚否 - 'Will you remarry?' Direct question about future marital status.
  • 不忍也 - 'Could not bear to,' expressing emotional resistance.
  • 服竟 - 'After the mourning period was complete,' referring to the end of the period of mourning for a deceased spouse.
  • 白日见其死妇语之 - 'Saw his dead wife in daylight speaking to him,' indicating a ghostly or supernatural encounter.
  • 君先结誓，云何负言 - 'You first made a vow, how could you break your word?' Rhetorical question expressing betrayal of a promise.
  • 因以刀割其阳道 - 'Thereupon, he cut off his penis with a knife,' literal and shocking statement, likely indicating castration or self-mutilation as punishment.
  • 人性永废 - 'Human nature is permanently ruined,' suggesting loss of moral or physical capacity.
  • 死数年 - 'Dead for several years,' indicating that the person has been deceased for a considerable time.
  • 作歌 - 'To compose or sing a song,' often used in classical Chinese to indicate a supernatural or prophetic utterance.
  • 笑甚有伦次 - 'Laughed very orderly,' suggesting strange or eerie behavior.
  • 当归不闻死复生 - Line from the song, likely alluding to the *Shijing* (Book of Poetry), expressing paradoxical idea of returning or resurrection.
  • 琅琊王凝之字叔平 - Formal naming convention: 'Wang Ningzhi of Langya, styled Shuping.'
  • 左将军夫人谢氏奕之女也 - 'Daughter of Xie Yi, wife of Left General,' indicating the woman’s noble lineage.
  • 频亡二男 - 'Lost two sons repeatedly,' indicating multiple losses.
  • 悼惜甚过 - 'Mourned and lamented excessively,' indicating extreme emotional grief.
  • 哭泣累年 - 'Cried for many years,' emphasizing prolonged sorrow.
  • 若居至艰 - 'As if in the deepest mourning,' metaphor for extreme grief.
  • 皆若锁械 - 'All as if bound in chains,' suggesting they are in some form of punishment or confinement.
  • 慰免其母 - 'Comfort and free your mother,' a request to cease mourning.
  • 宜自宽割 - 'Should you yourself be lenient and cut yourself off,' strange phrase suggesting self-restraint or sacrifice.
  • 可为作福 - 'Can be made to do good deeds,' indicating that the mother should perform acts of merit.
  • 哀痛稍止而勤功德 - 'Her sorrow slightly lessened, and she became diligent in meritorious deeds,' indicating a shift from grief to religious or moral practice.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 钅奏 - Likely '针' (zhēn), the needle. The loss of a needle could be a small incident with supernatural or symbolic meaning.
  • 吴兴袁乞妻 - Refers to a woman from Wu Xing (present-day Zhejiang), married to a man named Yuan Qi. The name '袁乞' may be a rare or misspelled variant.
  • 临终执乞手 - Common motif in Chinese literature of a dying spouse holding the hand of the surviving partner, often used to convey emotional farewell or final words.
  • 割其阳道 - Shocking act of self-mutilation, possibly symbolic of guilt, punishment, or a supernatural curse.
  • 临川聂包 - Person from Linchuan (present-day Jiangxi), who died many years ago and then returned as a ghost. Clear example of a ghost story, common in classical Chinese literature.
  • 南丰相沈道袭 - Local official or magistrate from Nancheng (Nanfeng), likely a historical figure or fictional character. The title '相' could mean 'assistant to the governor' or 'counsellor.'
  • 花上盈盈正闻行，当归不闻死复生 - Line appears to be a variation or allusion to classical poetry, possibly from the *Shijing* or *Yuefu* poetry. '当归' is a well-known term from *Shijing* and also a medicinal herb, often used in poetry to signify longing or return.
  • 琅琊王凝之 - Wang Ningzhi of Langya (modern Shandong) was a historical figure, a son-in-law of the famous Xie An, and a member of the elite scholarly class. He was known for his calligraphy and Daoist practices.
  • 左将军夫人谢氏奕之女 - Refers to the daughter of Xie Yi (a military general) and wife of a Left General. Reference to the powerful Xie family of the Eastern Jin dynasty, known for their political and cultural influence.
  • 频亡二男 - Loss of two sons, a common theme in classical Chinese literature, often used to explore themes of grief, fate, and spiritual intervention.
  • 哭泣累年 - Phrase used to describe prolonged mourning, often in the context of Confucian ideals of filial piety.
  • 锁械 - Refers to chains or restraints, often used in ghost stories to indicate that the spirits of the dead are bound or in some form of punishment.
  • 勤功德 - Refers to the practice of performing good deeds or religious acts to merit a better rebirth or to appease the spirits.

  • 琅琊王骋之妻陈郡谢氏生一男，小字奴子 - Describes the birth of a son to Lady Xie of Chenjun, wife of Wang Cheng of Langya, with the child given the nickname 'Nusi'.
  • 经年后王以妇婢招利为妾 - Indicates that after a year, Wang Cheng took a female servant named Zhaoli as his concubine.

  • 既窆反虞 - Refers to the ritual of returning to the house after the burial.
  • 舆灵入屋 - Describes the carriage carrying the coffin entering the house.
  • 凭儿忽于空中掷地 - Describes a sudden event where a spirit or ghost appears to throw something to the ground.
  • 便有嗔声曰：“何不作挽歌，今我寂寂上道耶！” - Describes the angry voice saying, 'Why don't you sing a lament, now I am silently going on the road!'
  • 骋之云：“非为永葬，故不具仪耳。” - Means that Wang Cheng said, 'It's not for a permanent burial, so the full ceremony is not held.'
  • 沙门有支法存者，本自胡人，生长广州，妙善医术，遂成巨富 - Introduces a Buddhist monk named Zhi Fachu, originally from the Western Regions, who grew up in Guangzhou and became wealthy through his medical skills.
  • 有八尺<翕毛>，光彩耀目，作百种形象 - Describes a magical item, possibly a talisman or amulet, that is eight chi long and emits dazzling light, capable of taking on various forms.
  • 又有沈香八尺板床，居常香馥 - Describes an eight chi long board bed made of agarwood, which always emits a fragrant aroma.
  • 太原王琰为广州刺吏，大儿邵之屡求二物，法存不与 - States that Wang Yan, governor of Guangzhou, and his eldest son, Shaozhi, repeatedly asked for these two items, but Zhi Fachu refused to give them.
  • 王因状法存豪纵，乃杀而籍没家财焉 - Means that Wang Yan accused Zhi Fachu of being arrogant and extravagant, and had him killed, confiscating his property.
  • 法存死后，形见于府内，辄打阁下鼓，似若称冤 - Describes how after Zhi Fachu's death, his spirit appeared in the government office, hitting the drum in the hall, as if complaining about his wrongful death.
  • 如此经日，王寻得病，恒见法存守之，少时遂亡 - Means that for several days, Wang Yan fell ill, constantly seeing Zhi Fachu's spirit guarding him, and eventually died.
  • 邵之比至扬都，亦丧 - States that Shaozhi, upon arriving in Yangdu, also died.
  • 河内司马惟之奴天雄死后还，其妇来喜闻体有鞭痕而脚著锁 - Describes how after Tianxiong, a servant of Sima Wei of Hedong, died, his wife, Laixi, heard that his body had marks of beating and was chained.
  • 问云：“有何过，至如此？”曰：“曾因醉窃骂大家，今受此罪。” - Describes Laixi asking what crime Tianxiong committed to deserve such punishment, and he replied that he had insulted the master while drunk.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 晋隆安中 - Temporal phrase meaning 'During the Long'an era of the Jin dynasty.' Standard way of indicating a time period in classical Chinese.
  • 夜梦人语云 - 'At night, he dreamed someone said...' Common narrative structure in classical Chinese storytelling.
  • 君何坏吾冢 - 'Why are you destroying my tomb?' Direct question in the dream, indicating the supernatural origin of the voice.
  • 设祭 - To set up a sacrifice or offering, a common ritual action in classical texts.
  • 今当移好处，别作小冢 - 'Now I will move to a better place, and make a small tomb.' A polite and respectful response to the spirit.
  • 明朝一人诣门求通 - 'The next morning, a man came to the door to request an audience.' Phrase used to introduce a supernatural or unexpected visitor.
  • 列坐乃言 - 'After sitting down, he said...' Indicates a formal or respectful manner of speaking.
  • 我居四十年 - 'I have lived here for forty years.' Statement of the spirit’s long occupancy of the tomb.
  • 昨蒙厚贶 - 'Yesterday I received a generous gift.' '厚贶' is a polite term for a generous gift or offering.
  • 相感何如 - 'How grateful I am to you!' Phrase expressing deep gratitude.
  • 吉日 - 'Favorable day' or 'auspicious day,' often used in rituals and omens.
  • 出棺 - 'To remove the coffin,' which implies the spirit is ready to be laid to rest.
  • 仆巾箱中有金镜 - 'I have three golden mirrors in my hat-case.' '巾箱' refers to a case for storing personal items, including mirrors.
  • 遂于棺头巾箱中取金镜三枚赠从 - 'Then he took three golden mirrors from the hat-case on the coffin and gave them to From.' Clear but archaic description of a supernatural gift.
  • 忽然不见 - 'Suddenly disappeared,' common ending for supernatural encounters in classical texts.
  • 司空 - A high-ranking official title, equivalent to a 'Minister of Works' or 'Chief Architect' in the Jin dynasty.
  • 郄方回 - A proper name; the surname is '郄' (Xi), and the given name is '方回.'
  • 葬妇于骊山 - 'Buried his wife in骊山 (Lishan).'骊山 is a famous mountain near Xi'an, historically associated with the Zhou and Qin dynasties.
  • 使会稽郡吏史泽治墓 - 'He sent the official Shi Ze of Kuaiji County to oversee the tomb construction.' '治墓' means to construct or manage a tomb.
  • 多平夷古坟 - 'He flattened many ancient tombs.' '平夷' means to level or flatten, and '古坟' refers to ancient burial mounds.
  • 构制甚伟，器物殊盛 - 'The structure was very grand, and the objects within were exceptionally rich.' Describes the tomb’s elaborate construction and valuable contents.
  • 冢发，闻鼓角声 - 'When the tomb was opened, the sound of drums and horns was heard.' Supernatural or ominous event.
  • 颍川诸葛闾字道明 - 'Zhu Ge Lu of Yingchuan, styled Dao Ming.' Standard classical format for introducing a person: surname, given name, and courtesy name.
  • 墓在扬州庄蒋山之西 - 'His tomb is located west of Zhuang Jiangshan in Yangzhou.' Gives the geographical location of the tomb.
  • 每至阴雨，冢中辄有弦歌之声 - 'Whenever it rains heavily, there are sounds of stringed instruments and singing from the tomb.' Supernatural phenomenon.
  • 朱文绣与罗子锺为友，俱仕于梁 - 'Zhu Wenxiu and Luo Zizhong were friends, both serving in the Liang dynasty.' Introduces two historical figures and their political affiliation.
  • 绣既死，子锺哭之，其夜亦亡 - 'After Zhu Wenxiu died, Luo Zizhong mourned him, and that night he also died.' Tragic and supernatural event.
  • 梁南七里有鸡山，绣葬于其中 - 'Seven li south of the Liang capital is Ji Mountain, where Zhu Wenxiu was buried.' Provides the burial site.
  • 北九里有雉涧，埋锺于其内 - 'Nine li north is Zhi Jian, where Luo Zizhong was buried.' Continues the geographical description.
  • 绣神灵变为鸡，锺魂魄化为雉 - 'Zhu Wenxiu’s spirit transformed into a chicken, and Luo Zizhong’s soul became a pheasant.' Symbolic transformation into birds.
  • 清鸣哀响，往来不绝 - 'Their clear calls and mournful sounds continue endlessly.' Describes the ongoing supernatural presence.
  • 故诗曰：鸡山别飞响，雉涧和清音 - 'Therefore, the poem says: 'Ji Mountain echoes with flying sounds, Zhi Jian harmonizes with clear tones.' Poetic conclusion to the story, using a couplet to summarize the supernatural phenomenon.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 晋隆安中 - Refers to the Long'an period (397–402 CE) of the Eastern Jin dynasty. Specific historical period in Chinese history.
  • 司空郄方回 - 'Xi Fanghui, the Minister of Works' – the title '司空' was one of the Three Excellencies in the Han and Jin dynasties, indicating a high-ranking official.
  • 骊山 - A famous mountain in Shaanxi province, historically associated with the Zhou and Qin dynasties, and also the site of the Mausoleum of the First Qin Emperor.
  • 会稽郡吏史泽 - 'Shi Ze, an official from Kuaiji County' – Kuaiji was a county in Zhejiang province, and this indicates a bureaucratic process in tomb construction.
  • 颍川诸葛闾 - 'Zhu Ge Lu of Yingchuan' – Yingchuan was a region in Henan, and the name '诸葛' is notable as it is the same as the famous strategist Zhuge Liang.
  • 扬州庄蒋山 - 'Zhuang Jiangshan in Yangzhou' – This is a geographical reference, but the exact location of this 'Jiangshan' is unclear and may be a local or fictional name.
  • 梁 - Refers to the Liang dynasty (502–557 CE), a Southern dynasties period in Chinese history.
  • 鸡山 - Named after the birds (chickens) that the spirit of Zhu Wenxiu transformed into, symbolizing their eternal presence in the landscape.
  • 雉涧 - Named after the pheasants that the soul of Luo Zizhong transformed into, symbolizing their eternal presence in the landscape.
  • 诗曰 - 'The poem says' – This indicates that the couplet is a classical poetic line, possibly from an anonymous or lost poem, used to summarize the supernatural phenomenon.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 时亦动摇，斫刺不陷 - Describes the object's movement and resistance to cutting or piercing, indicating its unusual nature.
  • 乃纵著江中，得水便有声，如雷向发长川 - Vivid description of the object's reaction when placed in water, likening its sound to thunder.

  • 僵尸人肉，堪为药军士分割之 - Describes the use of the body parts as medicinal substances, indicating a historical or cultural practice.

  • 方头漆棺，以朱题上云 - Describes the appearance of the coffin, with '方头漆棺' meaning 'square-headed lacquered coffin' and '以朱题上云' meaning 'with red inscriptions on top saying.'
  • 七百年堕水，元嘉二十载三月坠于悬 - Specific inscription on the coffin indicating its age and the date of its fall, with '元嘉二十载三月' meaning 'March of the 20th year of the Yuanjia era.'
  • 和盖从潮漂沈，辄溯流还依本处 - Describes the coffin's movement with the tide, returning to its original location.
  • 村人朱护等异而启之 - Describes the villagers' reaction and their opening of the coffin, with '异而启之' meaning 'finding it strange and opening it.'
  • 皤头著桂，鬓皓白 - Describes the appearance of the old woman, with '皤头著桂' meaning 'white-haired and wearing a wreath of osmanthus' and '鬓皓白' meaning 'white hair and beard.'
  • 不如生人，钗髻衣服，粲然若新 - Describes the old woman's appearance as lifeless but well-preserved, with '不如生人' meaning 'not like a living person' and '粲然若新' meaning 'brightly shining as if new.'
  • 送葬器物，枕履悉存 - Describes the presence of burial goods, pillows, and shoes, indicating a well-preserved tomb.
  • 护乃赍酒脯施于柩侧 - Describes the villagers offering wine and meat to the coffin, a ritual act.
  • 明觉，果得，即用改殓移于高阜 - Describes the outcome of the ritual, with '明觉，果得' meaning 'upon waking, indeed they found' and '即用改殓移于高阜' meaning 'and immediately used the money to re-inter the body on a high ground.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 尝梦得大象 - Literal description of a dream involving a large elephant, carrying symbolic meaning.
  • 以问万雅 - Means 'to ask Wan Ya,' where Wan Ya is likely a scholar or interpreter of dreams.
  • 君当为大郡守而不能善终 - A prophetic statement predicting both success and a tragic end.
  • 大象者，大兽也，取诸其音 - An explanation of the dream symbol, noting that '大象' (great elephant) is interpreted based on its sound.
  • 兽者守也 - A pun on '兽' (beast) and '守' (to guard), linking the elephant to the role of a governor.
  • 象以齿焚其身后，必为人所杀 - A proverbial saying about the elephant's tusks leading to its downfall, symbolizing the danger of power.
  • 执正不移 - Means 'to hold firm to righteousness without wavering.'
  • 敦遣沈充杀之而取其郡 - Describes the act of Wang Dun sending Shen Chong to kill Zhang Mao and take his position.
  • 晋安北将军司马恬于病中梦见一老翁曰 - A narrative introduction to a dream experienced by Sima Tian, the Anbei General of Jin.
  • 我邓公也，屋舍倾坏，君为治之 - The dream message from Deng Ai, requesting repairs to the temple.
  • 后访之，乃知邓庙，为立瓦屋 - Describes the discovery and subsequent repair of the temple.
  • 晋明帝时，献马者梦河神请之 - Introduces a dream about a horse being requested by a river god during the reign of Emperor Ming of Jin.
  • 及至，与帝梦同，遂投河以奉神 - Describes the fulfillment of the dream and the act of offering the horse to the river god.
  • 始，太傅褚褒亦好此马，帝云已与河神 - Indicates that the horse was also desired by Cao Cao's son, who was told it had been given to the river god.
  • 及褚公卒，军人见公乘此马矣 - Describes the supernatural appearance of Cao Cao riding the horse after his death.
  • 陶侃梦生八翼，飞翔冲天 - A dream of Tao Kan having eight wings and flying to the heavens.
  • 见天门九重，已入其入，惟一门不得进 - Describes the dreamer's journey through nine gates of heaven, reaching the last gate.
  • 以翼搏天 - The act of trying to push open the final gate with wings.
  • 阍者以杖击之，因堕地，折其左翼 - The final gate's guardian strikes the dreamer, causing him to fall and break his left wing.
  • 惊悟，左腋犹痛 - The dreamer wakes up, still feeling pain in his left side.
  • 其后都督入州，威果震主，潜有窥拟之志 - Describes Tao Kan's later career as a military governor, where he becomes powerful and ambitious.
  • 每忆折翼之祥，抑心而止 - The dreamer recalls the broken wing as a warning, leading him to restrain his ambitions.

  • 或言玄，融召令，一转便决，众咸骇服 - Means 'Someone mentioned Vang Hsuan, and Ma Rong summoned him to explain, and with one turn, he solved it, leaving everyone amazed and impressed.'
  • 及玄业成，辞归 - Means 'When Vang Hsuan completed his studies, he requested to return home.'
  • 融心忌焉，玄亦疑有追者 - Means 'Ma Rong felt jealous, and Vang Hsuan also suspected there might be pursuers.'
  • 乃坐桥下，在水上，据屐 - Means 'So he sat under a bridge, on the water, holding his shoes.'
  • 融果转式逐之，告左右曰：“玄在土下水上，而据木，此必死矣。” - Means 'Ma Rong indeed used divination to pursue him, telling his attendants, 'Vang Hsuan is below earth and above water, holding wood, so he must die.'
  • 遂罢追，玄竟以免 - Means 'So he gave up the pursuit, and Vang Hsuan was ultimately spared.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 介子推逃禄隐迹，抱树烧死 - Narrative describing Jie Zitui's act of hiding and ultimately burning to death while holding onto a tree.
  • 文公拊木哀嗟，伐而制屐 - Describes Duke Wen of Jin's emotional response and subsequent action of cutting down the tree to make shoes.
  • 每怀割股之功，俯视其屐曰：“悲乎，足下！” - Phrase where Duke Wen recalls Jie Zitui's sacrifice and uses '足下' (a respectful term for 'you') ironically.
  • 生及户损父 - Superstitious belief that a child born in May will harm their father.
  • 受命于天，岂受命于户 - Philosophical statement questioning the validity of the superstition.
  • 魏安禧王观翔雕而乐之 - Describes Wei Anxi Wang's enjoyment of watching a falcon fly.
  • 此有形无用者也 - Statement that the wooden falcon is useless.
  • 夫作无用之器，世之奸民也 - Moral judgment that creating useless objects is the act of a deceitful person.
  • 召隐游欲加刑焉 - Describes the king's intention to punish the craftsman.
  • 安知大王之恶此也 - Rhetorical question expressing the craftsman's surprise at the king's reaction.
  • 可谓知有用之雕鸟，未悟无用之雕鸟也 - Statement that the king understands the usefulness of living falcons but not the value of the wooden one.
  • 今臣请为大王翔之 - Statement of the craftsman's intention to demonstrate the wooden falcon's ability to fly.
  • 乃取而骑焉，遂翻然飞去，莫知所之 - Describes the craftsman riding the wooden falcon and flying away, leaving the king amazed.

  • 魏安禧王 - Wei Anxi Wang, a ruler of the Wei state during the Warring States period.
  • 雕 - A falcon, a bird of prey often used for hunting.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 慈烏來萃 - Means 'kind-hearted crows gather,' where '慈烏' refers to crows, often associated with filial piety in Chinese culture.
  • 欲令聋者远闻 - Means 'to make the deaf hear from afar,' a metaphorical expression indicating the desire for the message to reach far and wide.
  • 改葬 - Means 'reburial,' referring to the act of moving a deceased person's remains to a new location.
  • 為立碑焉 - Means 'to erect a stele there,' where '立碑' refers to setting up a monument or tablet.
  • 避难过吴 - Means 'to avoid trouble by going to Wu,' where '避过' means 'to avoid' and '吴' refers to the region of Wu.
  • 無诡妄也 - Means 'not false or deceptive,' where '無' means 'not' and '诡妄' means 'false' or 'deceptive.'
  • 刻石旁作“黄绢幼妇外孙齑臼”八字 - Means 'carved eight characters 'yellow silk young woman's grandson's mortar' on the side of the stone,' where '黄绢' refers to yellow silk, '幼妇' means young woman, '外孙' means grandson, and '齑臼' means mortar.
  • 离合义解之 - Means 'to interpret it using the method of combining and separating characters,' where '离合' refers to the method of combining and separating characters to form new words or phrases.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 汝南殷陶市同县张南宅 - Literally means 'Yin Tao from Runan bought a house in the same county as Zhang Nan's residence.' Introduces the main characters and their relationship.
  • 掘地得钱百万金千斤 - Means 'dug up and found a million dollars in gold and a thousand pounds of silver.' Describes a significant discovery.
  • 即以还南 - Means 'immediately returned it to Zhang Nan.' Indicates the immediate action taken by Yin Tao.
  • 君至德感神，宝为君出 - Means 'Your great virtue has moved the spirits, and the treasure has appeared for you.' Poetically praises Yin Tao's moral character.
  • 送付县 - Means 'sent it to the county office.' Indicates the formal handover of the treasure.
  • 河内荀儒字君林 - Means 'Xun Ru from Henei, courtesy name Junlin.' Introduces another character with his place of origin and courtesy name.
  • 乘冰省舅氏 - Means 'rode on the ice to visit his uncle.' Describes a specific action taken by Xun Ru.
  • 陷河而死 - Means 'fell into the river and died.' Indicates the tragic outcome of Xun Ru's journey.
  • 兄伦字君文 - Means 'his brother Lun, courtesy name Junwen.' Introduces Xun Ru's brother.
  • 求尸积日不得 - Means 'sought his body for many days but could not find it.' Indicates the prolonged search for Xun Ru's body.
  • 设祭冰侧 - Means 'set up a sacrifice by the side of the ice.' Describes a ritual performed by Xun Ru's brother.
  • 又笺与河伯投笺 - Means 'also wrote a letter to the River God and threw it in.' Indicates a further ritual action.
  • 一宿岸冰开，尸手执笺浮出 - Means 'after one night, the ice on the shore opened, and the body, holding the letter, floated out.' Describes the miraculous recovery of Xun Ru's body.
  • 伦又笺谢 - Means 'Lun also wrote a letter to thank the River God.' Indicates a final act of gratitude.
  • 蜀郡张贞行船覆溺死 - Means 'Zhang Zhen from Shu Prefecture drowned when his boat capsized.' Introduces another character and the circumstances of his death.
  • 贞妇黄因投江就之 - Means 'Zhang Zhen's wife Huang threw herself into the river to join him.' Describes the wife's act of devotion.
  • 积十四日，执夫手俱浮出 - Means 'after fourteen days, she held her husband's hand and they both floated out.' Describes the miraculous reunion of the couple.
  • 顺阳南乡杨丰与息名香于田获粟 - Means 'Yang Feng from Nanxiang, Shunyang, and his son Xiang were harvesting grain in the fields.' Introduces another character and his son.
  • 因为虎所噬 - Means 'were attacked by a tiger.' Describes the attack.
  • 香年十四，手无寸刃，直扌益虎颈，丰遂得免 - Means 'Xiang, aged fourteen, without any weapon, struck the tiger's neck, and Yang Feng was saved.' Describes the son's heroic act.
  • 香以诚孝至感，猛兽为之逡巡 - Means 'Xiang's sincere filial piety moved even the fierce beasts, causing them to hesitate.' Emphasizes the power of Xiang's virtue.
  • 太守平昌孟肇之赐贷之谷，旌其门闾焉 - Means 'Governor Meng Zhao of Pingchang granted them grain as a loan and honored their gate.' Indicates the recognition and reward given to the family.
  • 崔景贤为平昌郡守，有惠民政，尝悬一蒲鞭而未尝用 - Means 'Cui Jingxian, as the governor of Pingchang Prefecture, had benevolent governance and once hung a willow whip but never used it.' Describes Cui Jingxian's wise and gentle leadership.
  • 任城王六月沉饮，忽失所在，人以为中酒毒而化 - Means 'Prince Rencheng drank and disappeared in June, and people believed he was transformed by alcohol poisoning.' Describes a mysterious disappearance.
